role_summary: Pioneering Black journalist; Jet and Ebony Washington bureau chief from 1955; covered the September 1955 Emmett Till trial in Sumner, Mississippi, and the 1961 Freedom Rides through Anniston, Birmingham, and Montgomery. Born 1918, died 2017.
ai_reading: Simeon Booker shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 9 (catalog slug 'simeon-booker-and-moses-newson'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Simeon Booker is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 9 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Simeon Booker; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Simeon Booker is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 9
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Tallahatchie County Courthouse in Sumner, Mississippi
gallery.caption: The Tallahatchie County Courthouse in Sumner, Mississippi, site of the September 1955 Emmett Till murder trial that Booker covered for Jet.
gallery.photographer: Calvin Beale, U.S. Department of Agriculture
